title: Row counter — start
summary: Use a row counter and a short note to track progress.
steps: Write a note when color or stitch changes.
warnings: Do not count an unfinished row.
dontDo: do not reset without saving progress

title: Row counter — project system
summary: Offline system for rows, notes and project export.
warnings: Long projects need notes during work, not only at the end.
dontDo: do not rely only on memory

title: Abbreviation dictionary — basics
summary: Keep common abbreviations near the pattern.
steps: Check whether the pattern is PL, US or UK.
Make a small swatch if unsure.
warnings: Abbreviations can differ between languages and authors.
dontDo: do not assume every pattern uses the same terms

title: Abbreviation helper card
summary: Basic abbreviation card for beginner projects.
steps: PL: oł. means chain, psł. means single-type stitch, sł. means double-type stitch.
US: ch means chain, sc single crochet, dc double crochet.
UK terms may differ from US terms.
Copy abbreviations from your pattern into notes.
Save your own abbreviation card after ad.
warnings: US/UK crochet terms are often confused.
dontDo: do not mix US and UK terms blindly

title: Simple stitches — calm start
summary: Start with a chain and one basic stitch before a full pattern.
steps: Work one row with one stitch.
warnings: A first swatch may be uneven.
dontDo: do not pull loops too tight

title: Simple stitches — mini guide
summary: Offline practice card for basic crochet stitches.
steps: Start with a loose slip knot.
Practice one stitch type per row.
Use a marker at the row edge.
Write which hook size worked best.
warnings: A swatch is part of the project.

title: Scarf — beginner project
summary: A scarf is simple, repetitive and easy to track.
steps: Use row counter from the start.
warnings: Use markers if edges are uneven.
dontDo: do not start with very decorative yarn

title: Scarf — simple offline pattern
summary: Simple scarf plan with notes and row counter.
steps: Use one stitch for each row.
Add 1 to counter after every row.
Save row count and yarn used.
warnings: The MVP does not auto-calculate yarn usage.
dontDo: do not change hook size without note

title: Hat — careful start
summary: A hat needs size control and measuring.
warnings: Without a swatch the hat may be too small or large.

title: Hat — project plan
summary: Checklist for a simple hat with size control.
warnings: Yarn and hand tension strongly affect size.

title: Coaster — small project
summary: A coaster is good for tension and round counting.
warnings: Waving often means too many stitches.

title: Coaster — simple pattern
steps: Start with a small center or square.
warnings: Cotton is usually better for coasters than fluffy yarn.
dontDo: do not use material that handles heat badly

title: Amigurumi — marker start
summary: Amigurumi needs exact counting and a round marker.
warnings: Without marker you can lose the round start.

title: Amigurumi — project plan
summary: Offline system for a small figure.
steps: Write round number and stitch count.
warnings: Small figures are simple but require accurate counting.
dontDo: do not remove marker without moving it

title: Blanket — long project
summary: A blanket needs solid notes because it takes time.
warnings: You can forget hook size and yarn lot in long projects.
dontDo: do not start without material notes

title: Blanket — tracking plan
summary: Checklist for rows, squares or repeated motifs.
steps: Write yarn color, lot and hook size.
Choose tracking unit: rows, squares or motifs.
warnings: Different yarn lots may vary in shade.
dontDo: do not put project away without note

title: Basic crochet plan
summary: Write project, materials and tracking method first.
warnings: Offline tracking works only if progress is saved.
dontDo: do not start without material notes

title: Universal project card
summary: Offline system for a simple crochet project.
warnings: The MVP works offline and does not require AI.
dontDo: do not delete notes before export
